subject: Science
overall_learning_objectives: Understand ecosystem components, food chains/webs, and energy flow
Explain the 10% law and its implications for trophic levels
Describe biological magnification and its dangers
Analyze ozone layer depletion causes, effects, and protection measures
Differentiate biodegradable and non-biodegradable waste and discuss management
Apply concepts to real-life environmental issues through examples and discussions

lesson_plan.title: Ecosystem – Components and Structure
topics_covered: Biotic (producers, consumers, decomposers) and abiotic components
teaching_strategy: Lecture with diagrams and real-life examples
student_activities: Draw and label components of a pond ecosystem
List biotic and abiotic factors in school garden

lesson_plan.title: Food Chains, Food Webs, and Energy Flow
topics_covered: Food chain and food web differences
teaching_strategy: Explain energy loss with pyramid illustration
student_activities: Construct 2 food chains and convert to food web
Explain why top carnivores are fewer

lesson_plan.title: Biological Magnification and Ozone Depletion
topics_covered: Biomagnification: toxin accumulation in food chain
Ozone layer: role, depletion by CFCs, effects (UV increase)
teaching_strategy: Illustration of biomagnification with DDT example
student_activities: Draw biomagnification model with toxin levels

lesson_plan.title: Waste Management and Biodegradable Substances
topics_covered: Decomposition process and role of decomposers
5R principles: Reduce, Reuse, Recycle, Refuse, Recover

lesson_plan.title: Environmental Balance and Human Impact
topics_covered: Role of humans in disturbing balance
teaching_strategy: Group debate on development vs conservation
Link to ozone and waste issues
student_activities: Discuss one human activity disrupting balance

lesson_plan.title: Revision, Application Quiz, and Project Ideas
topics_covered: Recap full chapter: ecosystem to waste

teaching_aids: Charts: ecosystem, food web, ozone layer, waste types
Video clips on food chains/ozone depletion
teacher_tip: Use local examples (e.g., nearby pond/river pollution) to connect concepts. Encourage diagram practice for exams. Balance theory with discussions on current issues like plastic bans for relevance.
